Threat.Name: Privileged User Error
Threat.Description: A privileged user or non-person entity (NPE) improperly exercises or adversely affects the TOE, resulting in unauthorized services, ineffective security mechanisms, or unintended circumvention of security mechanisms.

Threat.Name: TSF Failure
Threat.Description: Security mechanisms of the TOE may fail, leading to a compromise of the TSF.

Threat.Name: Unauthenticated Transactions
Threat.Description: Relying parties within an information system depend on the TOE to accurately bind subjects to their credentials for use in authenticating and providing privacy for transactions. Without the proper binding provided by the TOE, relying parties cannot ensure adequate access controls on sensitive information, ensure transactional integrity, ensure proper accountability, and/or enforce non-repudiation.

Threat.Name: Unauthorized Access
Threat.Description: A malicious user, process, or external IT entity intentionally circumvents TOE security mechanisms.

Threat.Name: Unauthorized Update
Threat.Description: A malicious party attempts to supply the end user with an update to the product that may compromise the security features of the TOE.

Threat.Name: Undetected Actions
Threat.Description: Remote users or external IT entities may take actions that adversely affect the security of the TOE.

Threat.Name: User Data Reuse
Threat.Description: A malicious user, process, or external IT entity may gain access to user data that is not cleared when resources are reallocated.

Threat.Name: Weak Cryptography
Threat.Description: A weak hash or signature scheme may be compromised by an attacker and used to apply integrity checks to malicious content so that it appears legitimate.

Assumption.Name: No General Purpose Computing
Assumption.Description: It is assumed that there are no general-purpose computing capabilities (e.g., compilers or user applications) available on the TOE, other than those services necessary for the operation, administration and support of the TOE.

Assumption.Name: Physical Security
Assumption.Description: Physical security, commensurate with the value of the TOE and the data it contains, is assumed to be provided by the environment.

Assumption.Name: Trusted Administrator
Assumption.Description: TOE Administrators are assumed to follow and apply all administrator guidance in a trusted manner.

Policy.Name: Access Banner
Policy.Description: The TOE shall display an initial banner describing restrictions of use, legal agreements, or any other appropriate information to which users consent by accessing the TOE.

Objective.Name: Audit Loss Response
Objective.Description: The TOE will respond to possible loss of audit records when audit trail storage is full or nearly full by restricting auditable events.

Objective.Name: Audit Protection
Objective.Description: The TOE will protect audit records against unauthorized access, modification, or deletion to ensure accountability of user actions.

Objective.Name: Certificate Validity
Objective.Description: The TSF must ensure that certificates, certificate revocation lists, and certificate status information are valid.
Objective.AddressedBy: FDP_CER_EXT.1, FDP_CER_EXT.2, FDP_CER_EXT.3, FDP_CER_EXT.4, FDP_CRL_EXT.1, FDP_CSI_EXT.1, FDP_OCSPG_EXT.1, FDP_SDP_EXT.1, FDP_STG_EXT.1, FIA_CMCS_EXT.1, FIA_ESTS_EXT.1, FIA_X509_EXT.1, FIA_X509_EXT.2, FPT_NPE_EXT.1

Objective.Name: Configuration Management
Objective.Description: The TOE will conduct configuration management to assure identification of system connectivity (software, hardware, and firmware), and components (software, hardware, and firmware), auditing of configuration data, and controlling changes to configuration items.
Objective.AddressedBy: FDP_CER_EXT.1, FDP_CER_EXT.4, FDP_CRL_EXT.1, FDP_OCSPG_EXT.1, FMT_MOF.1(1), FMT_MOF.1(2), FMT_MOF.1(3), FMT_MOF.1(4), FMT_MOF.1(5), FMT_MTD.1, FPT_NPE_EXT.1

Objective.Name: Display Banner
Objective.Description: The TOE will display an advisory warning regarding use of the TOE.

Objective.Name: Integrity Protection
Objective.Description: The TOE will provide appropriate integrity protection for TSF data and software and any user data stored by the TOE.
Objective.AddressedBy: FCS_CDP_EXT.1, FCS_CKM_EXT.5, FDP_ITT.1, FPT_ITT.1, FPT_TST_EXT.1, FPT_TST_EXT.2

Objective.Name: Non-Repudiation
Objective.Description: The TOE will prevent a subscriber from avoiding accountability for sending a message by providing evidence that the subscriber sent the message; and control communications from unknown source.

Objective.Name: Protected Communications
Objective.Description: The TOE will provide protected communication channels for administrators, other parts of a distributed TOE, and authorized IT entities. The TOE will protect data assets when they are being transmitted to and from the TOE, including through intervening untrusted components.
Objective.AddressedBy: FCS_CDP_EXT.1, FCS_CKM.1, FCS_CKM.2, FCS_CKM_EXT.1, FCS_CKM_EXT.4, FCS_CKM_EXT.7, FCS_CKM_EXT.8, FCS_COP.1, FCS_HTTPS_EXT.1, FCS_IPSEC_EXT.1, FCS_RBG_EXT.1, FCS_STG_EXT.1, FCS_TLSC_EXT.2, FCS_TLSS_EXT.1, FDP_ITT.1, FIA_PSK_EXT.1, FPT_ITT.1, FPT_KST_EXT.1, FPT_KST_EXT.2, FPT_SKP_EXT.1, FPT_SKY_EXT.1, FPT_SKY_EXT.2, FTP_ITC.1, FTP_TRP.1

Objective.Name: Recovery
Objective.Description: The TOE will have the capability to store and recover to a previous state at the direction of the administrator (e.g., provide support for archival and recovery capabilities).

Objective.Name: Residual Information Clearing
Objective.Description: The TOE will ensure that any data contained in a protected resource is not available when the resource is reallocated.

Objective.Name: Session Lock
Objective.Description: The TOE will provide mechanisms that mitigate the risk of unattended sessions being hijacked.

Objective.Name: System Monitoring
Objective.Description: The TOE will provide the capability to generate audit data. The TOE will record in audit records: date and time of action and the entity responsible for the action.
Objective.AddressedBy: FAU_ADP_EXT.1, FAU_GEN.1, FAU_GEN.2, FAU_SAR.1, FAU_SAR.3, FAU_GCR_EXT.1, FAU_SCR_EXT.1, FAU_SEL.1, FAU_STG_EXT.1, FIA_UIA_EXT.1, FPT_STM.1

Objective.Name: TOE Administration
Objective.Description: The TOE will provide mechanisms to ensure that only privileged users are able to log in and configure the TOE, and provide protections for logged-in users. The TOE will ensure that administrative responsibilities are separated across different roles in order to mitigate the impact of improper administrative activities or unauthorized administrative access.
Objective.AddressedBy: FIA_AFL.1, FIA_PMG_EXT.1, FIA_UAU.7, FIA_UAU_EXT.1, FIA_UIA_EXT.1, FMT_MOF.1, FMT_MTD.1, FMT_SMF.1, FMT_SMR.2, FPT_APW_EXT.1, FTA_SSL_EXT.1, FTA_SSL.3, FTA_SSL.4

Objective.Name: TSF Self Test
Objective.Description: The TOE will provide integrity protection to detect modifications to firmware, software, and archived data.

Objective.Name: Verifiable Updates
Objective.Description: The TOE will provide the capability to help ensure that any updates to the TOE can be verified by the administrator to be unaltered and from a trusted source.

Objective.Name: Audit Generation
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides a mechanism for the generation of portions of the audit data.

Objective.Name: Certificate Repository
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides a certificate repository for storage of certificates (and optionally CRLs) issued by the TSF.

Objective.Name: Certificate Repository Search
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides the ability to search a certificate repository for specific certificate fields in certificates issued by the TSF and return the certificate and an identifier for the certificate that can be used to search the audit trail for events related to that certificate.

Objective.Name: Audit Retention
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides mechanisms for retention of audit records for both normal and extended retention periods.

Objective.Name: Audit Review
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides a mechanism for the review of specified audit data.

Objective.Name: Audit Storage
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides a mechanism for the storage of specified audit data.

Objective.Name: Cryptographic Services
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides cryptographic services that can be invoked by the TSF in order to perform security functionality.

Objective.Name: Key Archival
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides the ability to use split knowledge procedures to enforce two-party control to export keys necessary to resume CA functionality if the TSF should fail.

Objective.Name: No General Purpose Computing
Objective.Description: There are no general-purpose computing capabilities (e.g., compilers or user applications) available on the TOE, other than those services necessary for the operation, administration and support of the TOE.

Objective.Name: Physical Security
Objective.Description: Physical security, commensurate with the value of the TOE and the data it contains, is provided by the environment.

Objective.Name: Public Key Protection
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides protection for specified public keys associated with CA functions.

Objective.Name: Local Session Protection
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides the ability to lock or terminate local administrative sessions.

Objective.Name: Remote Session Protection
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides the ability to lock or terminate remote administrative sessions.

Objective.Name: TOE Administration Support
Objective.Description: The Operational Environment provides specified management capabilities required for the overall operation of a Certificate Authority, and the ability to restrict access to a subset of the capabilities as specified in the ST.

Objective.Name: Trusted Administrator
Objective.Description: The administrator of the TOE is not careless, willfully negligent or hostile, and administers the software within compliance of the applied enterprise security policy.

Objective.Name: Trusted Platform
Objective.Description: The operating system on which the TOE has been installed is securely configured, regularly patched, and not subject to unauthorized access.

Elements.Element: The TSF shall implement audit functionality and [selection: interface with auditing function(s) in the Operational Environment, no additional audit functionality] in order to perform audit operations on the following audit data: [assignment: Auditable events in Table 4 through Table 6 that require persistent storage].
The TSF shall [selection: store, invoke the Operational Environment to store] certificates and [selection: CRLs, no other information] issued by the TSF.

The TSF shall generate and [selection: invoke the Operational Environment to generate, no other actions] an audit record of: (a) Start-up of the TSF audit functions; (b) All auditable events for the [not specified] level of audit; (c) All administrative actions invoked through the TSF interface; (d) Specifically defined auditable events listed in Table 4 through Table 6.

The TSF shall [selection: include, invoke the Operational Environment to include] within each audit record at least: (a) Date and time of the event, type of event, subject identity, and the outcome (success or failure) of the event; (b) For each audit event type, information specified in column three of Table 4 through Table 6.

For audit events resulting from actions of identified users, the TSF shall be able to [selection: associate, invoke the Operational Environment to associate] each auditable event with the identity of the user that caused the event.
The TSF shall [prevent audited events, except those taken by the Auditor] and [assignment: other actions to be taken in case of audit storage failure] if the audit trail cannot be written to.
The TSF shall provide proof of origin for certificates it issues in accordance with the digital signature requirements using a mechanism in accordance with RFC 5280 and FCS_COP.1(2).

The TSF shall provide proof of origin for certificate status information it issues in accordance with the digital signature requirements in [selection: CRLs (RFC 5280), OCSP (RFC 6960), [assignment: other OCSP standards]] and FCS_COP.1(2).

The TSF shall require and verify proof of origin for certificate requests it receives [selection: CMC using mechanisms in accordance with FIA_CMCS_EXT.1, EST using mechanisms in accordance with FIA_ESTS_EXT.1].

The TSF shall require and verify proof of origin for public keys contained in certificate requests it receives via [selection: proof-of-possession mechanisms in CMC, proof-of-possession mechanisms in EST].

The TSF shall [selection: require and verify proof of origin for revocation requests it receives via [selection: CMC, EST using optional "full CMC" functionality], [assignment: support manual processes for revocation requests and responses]].

The TSF shall [selection: implement cryptographic functionality, invoke interfaces provided by the Operational Environment] in order to perform [selection: all, [selection: FCS_CKM.1, FCS_CKM.2, FCS_CKM_EXT.1(1), FCS_CKM_EXT.1(2), FCS_CKM_EXT.1(3), FCS_CKM_EXT.1(4), FCS_CKM_EXT.4, FCS_CKM_EXT.5, FCS_CKM_EXT.6, FCS_CKM_EXT.7, FCS_CKM_EXT.8, FCS_COP.1(1), FCS_COP.1(2), FCS_COP.1(3), FCS_COP.1(4), FCS_RBG_EXT.1, FCS_KSH_EXT.1]] cryptographic operations.

Persistent private and secret keys shall be stored within the [selection: TSF, Operational Environment] [selection: encrypted within a hardware rooted key hierarchy, in a hardware cryptographic module].
The TSF shall implement a certificate profile function and shall ensure that issued certificates are consistent with configured profiles.

The TSF shall generate certificates using profiles that comply with requirements for certificates as specified in IETF RFC 5280, ensuring: (a) version field contains integer 2; (b) issuerUniqueID or subjectUniqueID fields are not populated; (c) serialNumber unique per CA; (d) validity field notBefore/notAfter valid; (e) issuer field not empty; (f) signature and subjectPublicKeyInfo fields contain OID per FCS_COP.1(2); (g) extensions supported: subjectKeyIdentifier, authorityKeyIdentifier, basicConstraints, keyUsage, extendedKeyUsage, certificatePolicy; (h) null subject name accompanied by critical subjectAltName; (i) subjectKeyIdentifier unique per public key; (j) authorityKeyIdentifier matches issuer's subjectKeyIdentifier; (k) keyUsage and extendedKeyUsage consistent.
The TSF shall be able to generate at least 20 bits of random for use in issued certificates in [selection: serialNumber, notBefore, notAfter] fields, in accordance with FCS_RBG_EXT.1.
The TSF shall establish a linkage from certificate requests to issued certificates.
The TSF shall support the approval of certificates by [selection: RA, AOR, CA Operations Staff, rules] issued according to a configured certificate profile.

The TSF shall provide certificate status information whose format complies with [selection: ITU-T Recommendation X.509v1 CRL, ITU-T Recommendation X.509v2 CRL, the OCSP standard as defined by [selection: RFC 6960, other OCSP standard]].
The TSF shall support the approval of changes to the status of a certificate by [selection: RA, CA operations staff, rules].

The TSF and [selection: Operational Environment, no other component] shall ensure that any previous information content of a resource is made unavailable upon the [selection: allocation of the resource to, deallocation of the resource from] the following objects: [assignment: list of objects].

The TSF shall [selection: validate, interface with the Operational Environment to validate] certificates in accordance with: IETF RFC 5280 certificate validation and certificate path validation; certificate path must terminate with a certificate in the Trust Anchor Database; validate basicConstraints extension with cA flag set to TRUE for all CA certificates; validate revocation status using [selection: OCSP, CRL]; validate extendedKeyUsage field (Code Signing for updates, Client Authentication for TLS clients, Server Authentication for TLS servers).
The TSF shall only treat a certificate as a CA certificate if the basicConstraints extension is present and the CA flag is set to TRUE.

The TSF shall [selection: use, interface with the Operational Environment to use] X.509v3 certificates as defined by RFC 5280 to support authentication for code signing for TOE updates, [selection: IPsec, TLS, HTTPS, SSH], and [selection: integrity verification for TSF protected data, integrity verification for TSF software and firmware, [assignment: other uses], no additional uses].

When the TSF cannot determine the current revocation status of a certificate, the TSF shall [selection: allow the administrator to choose whether to accept the certificate, accept the certificate, not accept the certificate].
The TSF shall not establish a trusted communication channel if the peer certificate is deemed invalid.

The TSF shall [selection: provide, interface with the OE to provide] a [selection: password-based authentication mechanism, [assignment: other authentication mechanism(s)]] to perform privileged user authentication.

The TSF shall allow the following actions prior to requiring a non-TOE entity to initiate the identification and authentication process: Display the warning banner per FTA_TAB.1; Obtain certificate status information; [selection: download certificate from repository, no other actions, [assignment: list of services or actions]].

The TSF shall require each user to be successfully identified and authenticated before allowing any other TSF-mediated actions on behalf of that user, including subscriber certificate renewal, subscriber revocation requests, privileged user access, [selection: no other actions, [assignment: other TSF-mediated actions]].
For subscriber actions, the TSF shall verify that the DN of the certificate presented by the subscriber for authentication matches that of the certificate being affected by the subscriber's actions.

Requirement.Name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (Administrator Functions)

Elements.Element: The [selection: TSF, Operational Environment] shall restrict the ability to: (1) manage the TOE locally and remotely; (2) configure the audit mechanism; (3) configure and manage certificate profiles; (4) modify revocation configuration; (5) perform updates to the TOE; (6) perform on-demand integrity tests; (7) import and remove X.509v3 certificates into/from the Trust Anchor Database; [selection: (8) import secret and private keys; (9) configure CRL function; (10) configure OCSP function; (11) disable deprecated algorithms; (12) accept certificates whose validity cannot be determined; (13) [assignment: other security management functions]] to Administrators.

Requirement.Name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (CA/RA Functions)

Elements.Element: The [selection: TSF, Operational Environment] shall restrict the ability to: (1) approve and execute the issuance of certificates; (2) configure subscriber self-service request constraints; [selection: (3) configure automated certificate approval management; (4) approve rulesets for AORs; (5) accept, process and export CMC messages; (6) no other function] to [selection: CA Operations Staff, RA Staff].

Requirement.Name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (CA Operations Functions)

Elements.Element: The [selection: TSF, Operational Environment] shall restrict the ability to: (1) approve certificate revocation; [selection: (2) perform archival and recovery; (3) import a key share; (4) approve RA/AOR rulesets; (5) export PKCS#10 certificate request; (6) import CA certificate; (7) no other function] to CA Operations Staff.

Requirement.Name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (Admin/Officer Functions)

Elements.Element: The [selection: TSF, Operational Environment] shall restrict the ability to: (1) perform destruction of sensitive data when no longer needed; [selection: (2) participate as a second party for archival and recovery; (3) import a key share; (4) perform encrypted export of private or secret key or critical data] to [selection: Administrators, Auditor, CA Operations staff].

Requirement.Name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (Auditor Functions)

Elements.Element: The [selection: TSF, Operational Environment] shall restrict the ability to: Delete entries from the audit trail; [selection: Search the audit trail; Set or change the retention period parameter for audit records requiring extended retention] to Auditors.
The TSF shall restrict the ability to manage the TSF data to privileged users.

The [selection: TSF, Operational Environment] shall be capable of performing the following management functions: (1) Ability to manage the TOE locally and remotely; (2) Ability to perform updates to the TOE; (3) Ability to perform archival and recovery; (4) Ability to manage the audit mechanism; (5) Ability to configure and manage certificate profiles; (6) Ability to approve and execute the issuance of certificates; (7) Ability to approve certificate revocation; (8) Ability to modify revocation configuration; (9) Ability to configure subscriber self-service request constraints; (10) Ability to perform on-demand integrity tests; (11) Ability to destroy sensitive user data when no longer needed; (12) Ability to import and remove X.509v3 certificates into/from the Trust Anchor Database; [selection: (13-24) additional management functions].

The TSF and [selection: Operational Environment, no other component] shall maintain the roles: Administrator, Auditor, CA Operations Staff, [selection: RA Staff, Authorized Organizational Representative, no other roles].
The TSF and [selection: Operational Environment, no other component] shall be able to associate users with roles.

The TSF and [selection: Operational Environment, no other component] shall ensure that: No identity is authorized to assume both an Auditor role and any other role; No identity is authorized to assume both a CA Operations Staff role and any other role.

Requirement.Name: Failure with Preservation of Secure State

Elements.Element: The TSF shall preserve a secure state when the following types of failures occur: [selection: DRBG failure, signature verification failure, integrity test failure, integrity failure on audit, integrity failure on Trust Anchor database, [assignment: other potential TSF failures], [assignment: other potential Operational Environment failures]].
The TSF and [selection: Operational Environment, no other component] shall prevent the plaintext export of [assignment: list of all keys used by the TSF].
The TSF and [selection: Operational Environment, no other components] shall prevent unauthorized use of all TSF private and secret keys.
After [assignment: list of failures/service discontinuities] the TSF shall enter a maintenance mode where the ability to return to a secure state is provided.

The TSF shall [selection: implement, interface with the Operational Environment to implement] the ability to prevent reading of all pre-shared keys, private, and secret keys (e.g., KEKs, DEKs, session keys).
The TSF shall [selection: provide, interface with the Operational Environment to provide] reliable time stamps.
The TSF shall [selection: implement, interface with the Operational Environment to implement] the ability to check for updates and patches to the TOE.
The TSF shall [selection: implement, interface with the Operational Environment to implement] the ability to provide Administrators the ability to initiate updates to TOE firmware/software.

The TSF shall [selection: implement, interface with the Operational Environment to implement] the ability to verify firmware/software updates to the TOE using a digital signature prior to installing those updates.

The TSF shall [selection: implement, interface with the Operational Environment to implement] the ability to verify the digital signature whenever the software or firmware is externally loaded into the TOE and if verification fails, the TSF shall [assignment: action to be taken if the verification fails].

The TSF shall [selection: implement, interface with the Operational Environment to implement] the ability to allow privileged user-initiated termination of the privileged user's own interactive session.
Before establishing a privileged user session the TSF shall display an Administrator-configured advisory notice and consent warning message regarding unauthorized use of the TOE.

The TSF shall use [selection: HTTPS, IPsec, SSH, TLS] to provide a trusted communication path between itself and remote subscribers and privileged users that is logically distinct from other communication paths and provides assured identification of its end points and protection of the communicated data from modification, disclosure.
The TSF shall permit remote subscribers and privileged users to initiate communication via the trusted path.
The TSF shall require the use of the trusted path for initial subscriber and privileged user authentication and all remote administration actions.

Requirement.Name: Audit Review
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements audit review functionality

Elements.Element: The TSF shall provide Auditors with the capability to read all information from the audit records.
The TSF shall provide the audit records in a manner suitable for the Auditor to interpret the information.

Requirement.Name: Selectable Audit Review
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements audit review functionality

Elements.Element: The TSF shall provide the ability to apply searches of audit data based on [assignment: object identifier of certificate] associated with the event.

Requirement.Name: Certificate Repository Review
Requirement.Condition: If certificate repository search is provided by TSF

Elements.Element: The TSF shall [provide, invoke the Operational Environment to provide] the capability to search for certificates containing specified values of certificate fields.

Requirement.Name: Selective Audit
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements pre-selection of audit records

Elements.Element: The TSF shall be able to select the set of events to be audited by specific mechanisms from the set of all auditable events based on [selection: object identity, user identity, subject identity, host identity, event type] and [assignment: list of additional attributes].

Requirement.Name: Protected Audit Trail Storage (Normal)
Requirement.Condition: If audit data stored by the TOE

Elements.Element: The TSF shall protect the stored audit records in the audit trail from unauthorized deletion.
The TSF shall be able to prevent unauthorized modifications to the stored audit records in the audit trail.

Requirement.Name: Protected Audit Trail Storage (Extended)
Requirement.Condition: If extended audit records stored by the TOE

Elements.Element: The TSF shall protect the stored audit records in the extended retention audit trail from unauthorized deletion.
The TSF shall be able to prevent unauthorized modifications to the stored audit records in the extended retention audit trail.

Requirement.Name: External Audit Trail Storage
Requirement.Condition: If TSF initiates storage of audit data

Elements.Element: The TSF shall transmit generated audit data to an external IT entity using a trusted channel in accordance with FTP_ITC.1.

Requirement.Name: Extended Audit Trail Retention
Requirement.Condition: If TSF provides extended audit record retention

Elements.Element: The TSF shall [selection: store, invoke the Operational Environment to store] extended-retention audit records for [assignment: Administrator-configurable time period specified in days].

Requirement.Name: Proof of Receipt
Requirement.Condition: If CMC or EST selected

Elements.Element: The TSF shall provide proof of receipt for certificate requests in accordance with [selection: CMC (RFC 5272), EST (RFC 7030)].

Requirement.Name: Cryptographic Key Generation
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements cryptographic key generation

Elements.Element: The TSF shall generate cryptographic keys in accordance with specified algorithm and key sizes.

Requirement.Name: Cryptographic Key Establishment
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements cryptographic key establishment

Elements.Element: The TSF shall perform cryptographic key establishment in accordance with specified method.

Requirement.Name: Symmetric Key Generation for DEKs
Requirement.Condition: If DEKs are used for subscriber data protection

Elements.Element: The TSF shall [selection: generate, invoke interfaces provided by the Operational Environment to generate] data encryption keys (DEKs) of size [selection: 128-bit, 256-bit] using [selection: an RBG, a key generation capability of the OE, a TSF-provided mechanism that combines KEKs].

Requirement.Name: Cryptographic Key Zeroization
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements key zeroization

Elements.Element: The TSF shall zeroize plaintext secret and private cryptographic keys when no longer needed.

Requirement.Name: TSF Public Key Integrity
Requirement.Condition: If integrity mechanism selected for public key protection

Elements.Element: The TSF shall verify integrity of TSF public keys using [selection: digital signature per FCS_COP.1(2), keyed hash per FCS_COP.1(4)].

Requirement.Name: Cryptographic Operation (Symmetric Encryption/Decryption)
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements symmetric encryption

Elements.Element: The TSF shall perform symmetric encryption/decryption using AES with key sizes of [selection: 128, 256] bits and modes [selection: CBC, CCM, GCM, CTR, Key Wrap, Key Wrap with Padding] per ISO 18033-3, NIST SP 800-38A/C/D/E.

Requirement.Name: Cryptographic Operation (Digital Signature)
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements digital signature operations

Elements.Element: The TSF shall perform digital signature services in accordance with [selection: RSA per FIPS PUB 186-4, ECDSA per FIPS PUB 186-4] using key sizes of [selection: 2048, 3072, 4096 bits for RSA; 256, 384, 521 bits for ECDSA].

Requirement.Name: Cryptographic Operation (Hash Algorithm)
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements hash operations

Elements.Element: The TSF shall perform cryptographic hashing services in accordance with [selection: SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512] per ISO/IEC 10118-3:2004.

Requirement.Name: Cryptographic Operation (Keyed Hash Algorithm)
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements keyed hash operations

Elements.Element: The TSF shall perform keyed-hash message authentication in accordance with [selection: HMAC-SHA-1, HMAC-SHA-256, HMAC-SHA-384, HMAC-SHA-512] with key sizes [selection: 160, 256, 384, 512 bits] per ISO/IEC 9797-2:2011.

Requirement.Name: HTTPS Protocol
Requirement.Condition: If HTTPS selected in FTP_TRP.1

Elements.Element: The TSF shall implement HTTPS using TLS per FCS_TLSS_EXT.1 or FCS_TLSC_EXT.2.

Requirement.Name: IPsec Protocol
Requirement.Condition: If IPsec selected in FTP_TRP.1

Elements.Element: The TSF shall implement IPsec architecture as specified in RFC 4301.

Requirement.Name: Random Bit Generation
Requirement.Condition: If TSF implements random bit generation

Elements.Element: The TSF shall perform all deterministic random bit generation services in accordance with [selection: NIST SP 800-90A] using [selection: Hash_DRBG, HMAC_DRBG, CTR_DRBG (AES)].
The deterministic RBG shall be seeded by an entropy source that accumulates entropy from [selection: hardware-based noise source, platform-based RBG] with a minimum of 256 bits of entropy.

Requirement.Name: TLS Client Protocol
Requirement.Condition: If TLS selected and TOE acts as client

Elements.Element: The TSF shall implement TLS 1.2 (RFC 5246) supporting [selection: ciphersuites per RFC 5246, RFC 5289, RFC 5288].

Requirement.Name: TLS Server Protocol
Requirement.Condition: If TLS selected and TOE acts as server

Elements.Element: The TSF shall implement TLS 1.2 (RFC 5246) supporting [selection: ciphersuites per RFC 5246, RFC 5289, RFC 5288].

Requirement.Name: CRL Generation
Requirement.Condition: If CRLs selected in FCO_NRO_EXT.2.2

Elements.Element: The TSF shall generate CRLs conforming to RFC 5280.
The TSF shall sign CRLs with [selection: the CA signing key, a key specifically designated for CRL signing].

Requirement.Name: Basic Internal Transfer Protection
Requirement.Condition: If TSF is distributed across multiple physical platforms

Elements.Element: The TSF shall enforce the access control SFP to prevent the disclosure, modification of user data when it is transmitted between physically-separated parts of the TOE.

Requirement.Name: OCSP Response Generation
Requirement.Condition: If OCSP selected in FCO_NRO_EXT.2.2

Elements.Element: The TSF shall generate OCSP responses conforming to [selection: RFC 6960, [assignment: other OCSP standard]].

Requirement.Name: Authentication Failure Handling
Requirement.Condition: If password-based authentication selected

Elements.Element: The TSF shall detect when [selection: [assignment: positive integer number], an administrator configurable positive integer within [assignment: range of acceptable values]] unsuccessful authentication attempts occur related to [assignment: list of authentication events].
When the defined number of unsuccessful authentication attempts has been surpassed, the TSF shall [assignment: list of actions].

Requirement.Name: CMC Client Authentication
Requirement.Condition: If CMC selected in FCO_NRO_EXT.2.3

Elements.Element: The TSF shall perform authentication for certificate requests received via CMC using [selection: shared secret, certificate-based authentication, [assignment: other authentication mechanism]].

Requirement.Name: CMC Server Authentication
Requirement.Condition: If CMC selected in FCO_NRO_EXT.2.3

Elements.Element: The TSF shall support certificate requests via CMC in accordance with RFC 5272, RFC 5273, RFC 5274.

Requirement.Name: EST Client Authentication
Requirement.Condition: If EST selected in FCO_NRO_EXT.2.3

Elements.Element: The TSF shall perform authentication for certificate requests received via EST using [selection: shared secret, certificate-based authentication, [assignment: other authentication mechanism]].

Requirement.Name: EST Server
Requirement.Condition: If EST selected in FCO_NRO_EXT.2.3

Elements.Element: The TSF shall support certificate requests via EST in accordance with RFC 7030.

Requirement.Name: Password Management
Requirement.Condition: If password-based authentication selected

Elements.Element: The TSF shall provide the following password management capabilities: (a) Passwords shall be able to be composed of any combination of upper and lower case characters, digits, and special characters; (b) Minimum password length configurable by an administrator to at least 15 characters.

Requirement.Name: Pre-Shared Key Composition
Requirement.Condition: If IPsec with pre-shared keys selected

Elements.Element: The TSF shall be able to use pre-shared keys for IPsec connections.

Requirement.Name: Protected Authentication Feedback
Requirement.Condition: If password-based authentication selected

Elements.Element: The TSF shall provide only obscured feedback to the user while the authentication is in progress.

Requirement.Name: Protection of Administrator Passwords
Requirement.Condition: If password-based authentication selected

Elements.Element: The TSF shall store passwords in non-plaintext form.
The TSF shall prevent the reading of plaintext passwords.

Requirement.Name: Basic Internal TSF Data Transfer Protection
Requirement.Condition: If TSF is distributed across multiple physical platforms

Elements.Element: The TSF shall protect TSF data from disclosure, modification when it is transmitted between separate parts of the TOE.

Requirement.Name: Key Share Protection
Requirement.Condition: If split knowledge procedures implemented by TSF

Elements.Element: The TSF shall protect key shares using [selection: FCS_COP.1(1) encryption with a password-derived KEK per FCS_COP.1(5), FCS_COP.1(1) encryption with a KEK derived from [assignment: other derivation method], [assignment: other protection mechanism]].

Requirement.Name: Inter-TSF Trusted Channel
Requirement.Condition: If TSF communicates with external audit server or cryptographic module

Elements.Element: The TSF shall provide a communication channel between itself and [selection: an external audit server, an external cryptographic module, other IT entity] that is logically distinct from other communication channels and provides assured identification of its end points and protection of the channel data from modification and disclosure.
The TSF shall permit [selection: the TSF, another IT entity] to initiate communication via the trusted channel.
The TSF shall initiate communication via the trusted channel for [assignment: list of functions].

Requirement.Name: Cryptographic Operation (Password-Based Key Derivation Function)
Requirement.Description: Optional requirement for password-based key derivation

Elements.Element: The TSF shall perform Password-based Key Derivation Functions using HMAC-[selection: SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512] with output key sizes [selection: 128-bit, 256-bit] per NIST SP 800-132.

Requirement.Name: Non-X.509v3 Certificate Generation
Requirement.Description: Optional support for X.509 certificate formats other than v3

Elements.Element: For X.509 certificate formats other than v3, the TSF shall ensure these contain: Version (0 or 1); Unique identifier of the issuer; keyUsage; Unique identifier of the certificate; Validity period; Signature field per FCS_COP.1(2).

Requirement.Name: User Sensitive Data Protection
Requirement.Description: Optional requirement if subscriber PII is stored by the TOE

Elements.Element: The TSF shall protect [selection: subscriber identity information, subscriber contact information, photograph from official ID, background check information, copies of legal documents, captured biometrics, [assignment: other PII]] through encryption per FCS_COP.1(1) using a DEK.
The TSF shall destroy all protected data when no longer required using specified cryptographic data destruction method.

Requirement.Name: Public Key Protection
Requirement.Description: Optional requirement if TOE protects public keys used for CA functions

Elements.Element: The TSF shall use [selection: access controlled storage, an integrity mechanism] to protect the trusted public keys and certificates (trust store elements) used to validate local logon, trusted channel, and external communication to the CA.

Requirement.Name: NPE Constraints
Requirement.Description: Optional requirement for Non-Person Entity certificate management

Elements.Element: The TSF shall enforce an Administrator-configurable ruleset that specifies authorizations to submit NPE certificate requests.

The TSF shall require CA Operations Staff to register any RA, and shall require CA Operations Staff or authorized RA to register any AORs, and associate each AOR with an organization or set of devices prior to that AOR making requests on behalf of an assigned organization or devices.

Requirement.Name: Split Knowledge Procedures
Requirement.Description: Optional requirement if TOE enforces split knowledge procedures

Elements.Element: The TSF shall [selection: support, interface with the operational environment to support] split knowledge procedures to enforce two-party control for the export of CA signing keys and [selection: no other data, user private keys, [assignment: critical data or keys]] necessary to resume CA functionality after TSF failure using [selection: key sharing mechanisms, [assignment: other mechanism]].

Requirement.Name: TOE Integrity Test
Requirement.Description: Optional requirement if TOE provides software/firmware integrity testing

Elements.Element: The TSF shall apply a [selection: keyed hash per FCS_COP.1(4), digital signature per FCS_COP.1(2)] to the TOE software and firmware.
The [selection: TSF, Operational Environment] shall verify the integrity of the TOE software and firmware [selection: at power-up, at initialization, on-demand by a privileged user].

Requirement.Name: Integrity Test
Requirement.Description: Optional requirement if TSF provides integrity protection for TSF data

Elements.Element: The TSF shall apply a [selection: keyed hash per FCS_COP.1(4), digital signature per FCS_COP.1(2)] to the [selection: Trust Anchor Database element(s), TSF keys used to manage certificates, certificate database, [assignment: other data relevant to TSF security]].
Integrity shall be verified at [selection: power-up, initialization, on-demand by a privileged user].

Requirement.Name: TSF-Initiated Termination
Requirement.Description: Optional requirement if TSF implements remote session termination after inactivity

Elements.Element: The TSF shall terminate a remote interactive session after a [assignment: Administrator-configurable time interval of session inactivity].

Requirement.Name: TSF-Initiated Session Locking
Requirement.Description: Optional requirement if TSF implements local session locking/termination after inactivity

Elements.Element: The TSF shall, for local interactive sessions, [selection: lock the session, terminate the session] after an Administrator-configured time period of inactivity.

Requirement.Name: Basic Functional Specification
Requirement.Description: Developer shall provide a functional specification and tracing from functional specification to SFRs. The functional specification shall describe purpose and method of use for each SFR-enforcing and SFR-supporting TSFI, identify all parameters, and provide rationale for implicit categorization of interfaces as SFR-non-interfering.

Requirement.Name: Operational User Guidance
Requirement.Description: Developer shall provide operational user guidance that describes for each privileged user role: user-accessible functions and privileges, how to use interfaces securely, available functions and security parameters, security-relevant events, all possible modes of operation, and security measures for fulfilling security objectives.

Requirement.Name: Preparative Procedures
Requirement.Description: Developer shall provide preparative procedures describing all steps necessary for secure acceptance of the delivered TOE and secure installation of the TOE and preparation of the operational environment.

Requirement.Name: Labeling of the TOE
Requirement.Description: Developer shall provide the TOE and a reference for the TOE. The TOE shall be labeled with its unique reference.

Requirement.Name: TOE CM Coverage
Requirement.Description: Developer shall provide a configuration list for the TOE that includes the TOE itself and the evaluation evidence required by the SARs. The configuration list shall uniquely identify the configuration items.

AssuranceClass.name: Security Target Evaluation
AssuranceClass.Description: As per activities defined in Common Evaluation Methodology (CEM).

Requirement.Name: Independent Testing - Conformance
Requirement.Description: Developer shall provide the TOE for testing. The TOE shall be suitable for testing. Evaluator shall prepare a test plan and report documenting testing aspects, covering all CEM and PP Assurance Activities requirements.

Requirement.Name: Vulnerability Survey
Requirement.Description: Developer shall provide the TOE for testing. Evaluator shall perform a search of public domain sources to identify potential vulnerabilities and conduct penetration testing to determine that the TOE is resistant to attacks performed by an attacker possessing Basic attack potential.

Role.Description: Responsible for management activities, including configuration of the CA and its security functions.
Responsible for reviewing and managing audit records. Must be separate from other roles.
CA Operations Staff responsible for certificate issuance approval, revocation, and operational functions. Must be separate from other roles.
Registration Authority Staff responsible for validating subscriber identity before instructing the CA to manipulate certificates.
Authorized Organizational Representative delegated authority to manage a restricted set of certificates associated to devices belonging to a particular organization.

ReferenceStandards.Standard: Certificate Management over CMS (CMC)
CMC: Transport Protocols
CMC: Compliance Requirements
Internet X.509 Public Key Infrastructure Certificate and CRL Profile
Transport Layer Security (TLS) Protocol Version 1.2
AES Galois Counter Mode (GCM) Cipher Suites for TLS
TLS Elliptic Curve Cipher Suites with SHA-256/384
X.509 Internet Public Key Infrastructure Online Certificate Status Protocol - OCSP
Enrollment over Secure Transport (EST)
Security Architecture for the Internet Protocol
Digital Signature Standard (DSS)
Recommendation for Block Cipher Modes of Operation
Recommendation for Block Cipher Modes of Operation: CCM Mode
Recommendation for Block Cipher Modes of Operation: GCM Mode
Recommendation for Block Cipher Modes of Operation: XTS-AES Mode
Recommendation for Random Number Generation Using Deterministic RBGs
Recommendation for Key Derivation Using Pseudorandom Functions
Recommendation for Password-Based Key Derivation
Encryption algorithms - Part 3: Block ciphers
Reference Certificate Policy

MappingMetadata.Title: Mapping Between Protection Profile for Certification Authorities, Version 2.1 and NIST SP 800-53 Revision 5
MappingMetadata.SourcePP: Protection Profile for Certification Authorities, Version 2.1, 1-December-2017

Caveats.Caveat: Product vs. System: Common Criteria evaluates products; RMF (NIST SP 800-37 Rev 2, DoD 8510.01) and associated controls (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5, CNSSI 1253) are used for system assessment and authorization. Products cannot satisfy controls outside system context.

IA-4, IA-5(2), and SC-17: The primary purpose of a CA product is to handle distribution and maintenance of identifiers in support of PKI infrastructure. A conformant TOE supports system satisfaction of these controls at a general level.
SA-4(7): Satisfaction of any NIAP PP supports system satisfaction of SA-4(7), which implements CNSSP No. 11.

System Context: For a conformant TOE to support controls in an information system context, selections and assignments in the Security Target must be congruent with those made for the supported controls.

TOE vs OE Implementation: Many SFRs describe functionality that may be implemented by the TOE or through TSF invocation of a similarly-validated component in the operational environment. Such SFRs are denoted with asterisk (*).

Control.name: Event Logging
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to generate audit records for various events. It may also make use of the operational environment to generate a subset of the required audit records.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE partially supports the enforcement of this control by storing certificates and potentially CRLs using either TSF or environmental mechanisms.

Control.name: Event Logging
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to generate audit records for various events. It may also make use of the operational environment to generate a subset of the required audit records. The TOE supports the enforcement of the control if its auditable events are consistent with the assignments chosen for the control and if the TOE's audit log is part of the overall system's auditing.

Control.name: Content of Audit Records
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will ensure that audit records include date, type, outcome, and subject identity data. The TOE supports the enforcement of the control if its auditable events are consistent with the assignments chosen for the control and if the TOE's audit log is part of the overall system's auditing.

Control.name: Content of Audit Records: Additional Audit Information
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will ensure that audit records include date, type, outcome, and subject identity data. It may also make use of the operational environment to satisfy this control.

Control.name: Audit Record Generation
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to generate audit logs. The TOE supports the enforcement of parts (a) and (c) of the control if its auditable events are consistent with the assignments chosen for the control. Part (b) is not satisfied by a conformant TOE because the PP does not define functionality to suppress/enable the generation of specific audit records.

Control.name: Content of Audit Records
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will ensure that audit records include date, type, outcome, and subject identity data. The TOE supports the enforcement of the control if its auditable events are consistent with the assignments chosen for the control and if the TOE's audit log is part of the overall system's auditing.

Control.name: Audit Log Storage Capacity
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by allocating storage for audit data.

Control.name: Response to Audit Logging Process Failures
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of part (b) of this control by taking some action in the event that audit data cannot be written. This SFR does not require an alerting method so part (a) is not applicable.

Control.name: Non-repudiation
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE enforces non-repudiation through verification of certificate origin.

Control.name: Non-repudiation: Association of Identities
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of part (a) of this control through its binding of its issued certificates. It supports the enforcement of part (b) of this control by associating its own identity with its issued certificates.

Control.name: Non-repudiation: Validate Binding of Information Producer Identity
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE may support this control by having a mechanism for verifying proof of origin for revocation requests.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: Depending on the selections made in this SFR, a conformant TOE or its operational environment supports the enforcement of this control. Specific control applicability for each selection can be found in the mappings for the individual SFRs that the selection items represent.

Control.name: Cryptographic Protection
Control.Comment: Depending on the selections made in this SFR, a conformant TOE or its operational environment supports the enforcement of this control.

Control.name: Authenticator Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE protects private key data used as authenticators from unauthorized disclosure, either through its own mechanisms or through environmental ones, in support of part (h) of this control.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by protecting stored cryptographic data, either through its own mechanisms or through invocation of environmental ones.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE partially supports the enforcement of this control by defining parameters for certificate generation.

Control.name: Public Key Infrastructure Certificates
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE implements a certificate profile function and ensures that certificates are generated following the policy defined by that function.

Control.name: Non-repudiation
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the implementation of this control through association of certificates and the entities that request them.

Control.name: Public Key Infrastructure Certificates
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE implements a certificate profile function and ensures that certificates are approved in accordance with the policy defined by that function.

Control.name: Session Authenticity: Allowed Certificate Authorities
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by allowing the TSF to act as an allowed CA for approving certificates.

Control.name: Authenticator Management: Public Key-Based Authentication
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports part (b) of this control through a mechanism that allows external entities to determine whether the presented certificate is valid.

Control.name: Public Key Infrastructure Certificates
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the issuance of PKI certificates by generating status information about the certificates it has issued.

Control.name: Information in Shared System Resources
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE ensures, either through its own mechanisms or through invocation of its operational environment, that residual data stored in data buffers is purged prior to re-use of those buffers.

Control.name: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity: Pre- and Post-Transmission Handling
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE and potentially the operational environment ensures that any previous information content of certificate authority functions and network communications is made unavailable before reuse.

Control.name: Authenticator Management: Public Key-Based Authentication
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to validate certificate path and status or invoke an environmental service to do this, which satisfies this control.

Control.name: Session Authenticity
Control.Comment: Depending on the TOE's use of trusted communications channels, it may use X.509 certificate validation in support of session authentication.

Control.name: Session Authenticity: Allowed Certificate Authorities
Control.Comment: If the TOE uses X.509 certificates as part of session authentication, it will include the functionality needed to validate certificate authorities or invoke this functionality in its operational environment.

Control.name: Signed Components
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by using code signing certificates for software updates or invokes this functionality in its operational environment.

Control.name: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to identify and authenticate organizational users via X.509 certificates.

Control.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity: Code Authentication
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE's use of a code signing certificate for software updates supports the enforcement of this control. It may make use of the operational environment to support the enforcement of this control.

Control.name: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to provide an authentication mechanism for privileged users, or to interface with its operational environment to provide this.

Control.name: Permitted Actions without Identification or Authentication
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will define a list of actions that are permitted prior to authentication.

Control.name: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to require that certain functions require successful authentication to access, whether provided by the TOE itself or its environment.

SFRMapping.name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (Administrator Functions)

Control.name: Access Enforcement: Role-Based Access Control
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by specifying the management functions that are available to the Administrator role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment enforces least privilege by defining the minimum set of relevant management functions that are available to the Administrator role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege: Authorize Access to Security Functions
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment defines its management authorizations such that explicit authorization is required to perform a management function.

SFRMapping.name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (CA/RA Functions)

Control.name: Access Enforcement: Role-Based Access Control
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment supports the enforcement of this control by specifying the management functions that are available to the CA Operations Staff or RA Staff role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment enforces least privilege by defining the minimum set of relevant management functions that are available to the CA Operations Staff or RA Staff role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege: Authorize Access to Security Functions
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment defines its management authorizations such that explicit authorization is required to perform a management function.

SFRMapping.name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (CA Operations Functions)

Control.name: Access Enforcement: Role-Based Access Control
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment supports the enforcement of this control by specifying the management functions that are available to the CA Operations Staff role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment enforces least privilege by defining the minimum set of relevant management functions that are available to the CA Operations Staff role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege: Authorize Access to Security Functions
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment defines its management authorizations such that explicit authorization is required to perform a management function.

SFRMapping.name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (Admin/Officer Functions)

Control.name: Access Enforcement: Role-Based Access Control
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment supports the enforcement of this control by specifying the management functions that are available to the Administrator, Auditor, or CA Operations Staff role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment enforces least privilege by defining the minimum set of relevant management functions that are available to the Administrator, Auditor, or CA Operations Staff role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege: Authorize Access to Security Functions
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment defines its management authorizations such that explicit authorization is required to perform a management function.

SFRMapping.name: Management of Security Functions Behavior (Auditor Functions)

Control.name: Access Enforcement: Role-Based Access Control
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment supports the enforcement of this control by specifying the management functions that are available to the Auditor role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment enforces least privilege by defining the minimum set of relevant management functions that are available to the Auditor role as opposed to other defined roles.

Control.name: Least Privilege: Authorize Access to Security Functions
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment defines its management authorizations such that explicit authorization is required to perform a management function.

Control.name: Access Enforcement: Role-Based Access Control
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports this control by restricting the management of TSF data to privileged users.

Control.name: Least Privilege
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports this control by restricting the management of TSF data to privileged users.

Control.name: Least Privilege: Authorize Access to Security Functions
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports this control by restricting the management of TSF data to privileged users.

Control.name: Configuration Settings
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment may satisfy one or more optional capabilities defined in this SFR. In general, a conformant TOE will satisfy this control to the extent that the TOE provides a method to configure its behavior in accordance with organizational requirements.

Control.name: Account Management: Privileged User Accounts
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE and potentially its operational environment maintains roles and is able to associate users with roles, in support of part (a) of the control.

Control.name: Separation of Duties
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or potentially its operational environment enforces separation of duties of individuals and roles.

SFRMapping.name: Failure with Preservation of Secure State

Control.name: Fail in Known State
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE preserves a secure state in the event of a failure.

Control.name: Media Transport
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE and optionally the operational environment prevents the export of plaintext keys, including to any physical media attached to the system on which the TOE is running.

Control.name: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by ensuring that key data exported from the TOE is protected. It may also make use of the operational environment to provide this functionality.

Control.name: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity: Cryptographic Protection
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by ensuring that key data exported from the TOE is encrypted. It may also make use of the operational environment to provide this functionality.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by ensuring the protection of stored cryptographic data. It may also make use of the operational environment to provide this functionality.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by ensuring the protection of stored cryptographic data. It may also make use of the operational environment to provide this functionality.

Control.name: System Recovery and Reconstitution
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by providing a mechanism for itself to be restored to a secure state following a failure.

Control.name: Safe Mode
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control through its support of a maintenance mode, which is equivalent to the 'safe mode' specified by this control.

Control.name: Access Enforcement: Restrict Access to Specific Information Types
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE restricts access to the key storage repository, which supports this control if such a repository is identified by the organization as requiring restricted access.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by protecting stored cryptographic data, either through the TSF or through reliance on the operational environment.

Control.name: Time Stamps
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE can generate or use time stamps to address the actions defined in this control.

Control.name: System Time Synchronization: Synchronization with Authoritative Time Source
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE may have the ability to synchronize with an NTP server in its operational environment, satisfying this control.

Control.name: Signed Components
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE requires that updates to it include digital signatures for integrity measures, either through the TSF or through reliance on the operational environment.

Control.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity: Integrity Checks
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to verify the integrity of updates to it, either through the TSF or through reliance on the operational environment.

Control.name: Session Termination: User-Initiated Logouts
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to terminate an active session upon user request, either through the TSF or through reliance on the operational environment.

Control.name: System Use Notification
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE displays an advisory warning to the user prior to authentication.

Control.name: Device Identification and Authentication: Cryptographic Bidirectional Authentication
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE may support the enforcement of this control if the protocol(s) used to establish trusted communications uses mutual authentication.

Control.name: Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity: Cryptographic Protection
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will have the ability to prevent unauthorized disclosure of information and detect modification to that information.

Control.name: Trusted Path
Control.Comment: The TOE establishes a trusted communication path between remote users and itself.

Control.name: Cryptographic Protection
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to perform or invoke environmental services for key derivation using NSA-approved and FIPS-validated algorithms.

Control.name: Public Key Infrastructure Certificates
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE implements a certificate profile function and ensures that X.509 certificates in formats other than v3 are generated following the policy defined by that function.

Control.name: Protection of Information at Rest
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment has the ability to protect sensitive information at rest. This supports the enforcement of the control through use by the Privacy Overlay to protect PII.

Control.name: Access Enforcement
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will use access control or integrity protection to protect key and certificate data.

Control.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will use access control or integrity protection to protect key and certificate data.

Control.name: Authenticator Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports part (h) of this control by ensuring the confidentiality or integrity of stored public key and certificate data.

Control.name: Public Key Infrastructure Certificates
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE enforces a certificate issuance policy through an administrator-configurable ruleset that specifies authorizations to submit non-person entity certificate requests.

Control.name: Access Enforcement: Dual Authorization
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by requiring split knowledge procedures for export of key data, either through its own functionality or by interfacing with its operational environment.

Control.name: System Backup
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of part (b) of this control because the key data being exported from the TOE is system-level information that is being stored externally in the case that a recovery from failure is needed. Part (d) is enforced through the use of split knowledge procedures that ensure the confidentiality of this data is maintained while outside the TOE boundary.

Control.name: Security and Privacy Function Verification
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment will run automatic tests to ensure correct operation of its own functionality.

Control.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment supports the enforcement of this control by including an integrity checking mechanism for itself.

Control.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity: Integrity Checks
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment supports the enforcement of this control by including an integrity checking mechanism for itself.

Control.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity: Integrity Verification
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or the operational environment supports the enforcement of this control by providing an integrity verification mechanism for the TOE software.

Control.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by including an integrity checking mechanism for its stored data.

Control.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity: Integrity Checks
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by including an integrity checking mechanism for its stored data.

Control.name: Account Management: Inactivity Logout
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will have the ability to log out after a period of inactivity.

Control.name: Session Termination
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will have the ability to terminate an idle remote interactive session.

Control.name: Device Lock
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE may have the ability to lock an idle local interactive session, depending on the selection made in the SFR.

Control.name: Session Termination
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE may have the ability to terminate an idle local interactive session, depending on the selection made in the SFR.

Control.name: Re-authentication
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE may have the ability to require user re-authentication after the termination of an idle local interactive session, depending on the selection made in the SFR.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by using a mechanism to generate DEKs, either through the TSF or through reliance on the operational environment.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management: Symmetric Keys
Control.Comment: The TOE generates or interfaces with the operational environment to generate DEKs, which are symmetric keys.

Control.name: Not Applicable
Control.Comment: This function allows searches to be performed for certificate data based on specified fields. It does not relate to the enforcement of any security controls.

Control.name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting: Permitted Actions
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by limiting access to the audit review function to members of the Auditor role.

Control.name: Audit Reduction and Report Generation
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by providing a mechanism to read audit information.

Control.name: Audit Reduction and Report Generation
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by providing a mechanism to review stored audit data.

Control.name: Audit Reduction and Report Generation: Automatic Processing
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by allowing for the definition of audit search criteria that can be used to search for and read an auditor-defined subset of the audit records.

Control.name: Audit Record Generation
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports part (b) of this control by allowing authorized users to specify the auditable events that the TSF will generate.

Control.name: Protection of Audit Information
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to prevent unauthorized modification and deletion of audit records.

Control.name: Protection of Audit Information: Read-Only Access
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to prevent unauthorized modification and deletion of audit records. If the TOE prevents this by preventing all modification and deletion of audit records, it can be used to support the enforcement of this control.

Control.name: Protection of Audit Information
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to prevent unauthorized modification and deletion of audit records.

Control.name: Protection of Audit Information: Read-Only Access
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to prevent unauthorized modification and deletion of audit records. If the TOE prevents this by preventing all modification and deletion of audit records, it can be used to support the enforcement of this control.

Control.name: Audit Record Retention
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by defining 'archive' audit records that can have extended retention requirements in accordance with organizational procedures.

Control.name: Audit Log Storage Capacity
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE shall maintain availability and integrity of audit data by storing it locally on the TOE or TOE platform, or on an external IT entity using a trusted channel protocol.

Control.name: Audit Log Storage Capacity: Transfer to Alternate Storage
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to transmit audit data to a location in its operational environment using a trusted channel protocol.

Control.name: Protection of Audit Information
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to prevent unauthorized modification and deletion of audit records.

Control.name: Protection of Audit Information: Store on Separate Physical Systems or Components
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE has the ability to transmit audit data to a location in its operational environment using a trusted channel protocol.

Control.name: Audit Record Retention
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by defining 'archive' audit records that can have extended retention requirements in accordance with organizational procedures.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: The ability of the TOE to generate asymmetric keys or invoke interfaces provided by the operational environment to generate asymmetric keys satisfies the key generation portion of this control.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management: Asymmetric Keys
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE ensures that generated asymmetric keys or the invoking interfaces provided by the operational environment to generated asymmetric keys provide an appropriate level of security.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or interfaces provided by the operational environment supports this control by providing a key establishment function.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management: Asymmetric Keys
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE or interfaces provided by the operational environment supports the production of asymmetric keys by providing a key establishment function.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the key generation portion of this control by generating, or interfacing with its operational environment to generate, key encryption keys. The TOE will also support SC-12(2) or SC-12(3), depending on whether it uses symmetric or asymmetric keys for this purpose.

SFRMapping.name: Key Generation for Key Encryption Keys (TOE Key Archival)

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the key generation portion of this control by generating, or interfacing with its operational environment to generate, key encryption keys. The TOE will also support SC-12(2) or SC-12(3), depending on whether it uses symmetric or asymmetric keys for this purpose.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the key generation portion of this control by generating, or interfacing with its operational environment to generate, key shares. The TOE will also support SC-12(2) or SC-12(3), depending on whether it uses symmetric or asymmetric keys for this purpose.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE securely destroys or interfaces with the operational environment to securely destroy cryptographic keys.

Control.name: Software, Firmware, and Information Integrity: Cryptographic Protection
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by using cryptographic methods to protect the integrity of stored key data either through the TSF or through reliance on the operational environment.

Control.name: System Recovery and Reconstitution
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by providing a mechanism, either internal to the TOE or invoked from its operational environment, to import/export a key archive to maintain continuity of operations in the event of a TSF failure.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the enforcement of this control by protecting the stored key archive, either within the TSF or through invocation of an environmental interface.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE supports the key generation portion of this control by generating, or interfacing with its operational environment to generate, a root encryption key. The TOE will also support SC-12(2) or SC-12(3), depending on whether it uses symmetric or asymmetric keys for this purpose.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will use a key hierarchy to ensure the secure storage of cryptographic keys.

Control.name: Cryptographic Protection
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE performs or interfaces with the operational environment to perform symmetric encryption and decryption using NSA-approved and FIPS-validated algorithms.

Control.name: Cryptographic Protection
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE performs or interfaces with the operational environment to perform cryptographic signing using NSA-approved and FIPS-validated algorithms.

Control.name: Cryptographic Protection
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE performs or interfaces with the operational environment to perform cryptographic hashing using NSA-approved and FIPS-validated algorithms.

Control.name: Cryptographic Protection
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE performs or interfaces with the operational environment to perform keyed-hash message authentication using NSA-approved and FIPS-validated algorithms.

Control.name: Cryptographic Key Establishment and Management
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE's use of an appropriate DRBG ensures that generated keys provide an appropriate level of security either through the TSF or through reliance on the operational environment.

Control.name: Unsuccessful Logon Attempts
Control.Comment: The TOE or through reliance on the operational environment has the ability to detect when a defined number of unsuccessful authentication attempts occur and take some corrective action.

Control.name: Authenticator Management: Password-Based Authentication
Control.Comment: A conformant TOE will have the ability to enforce some minimum password complexity requirements, although they are not identical to CNSS or DoD requirements or to those specified in part (a) of this control.

Control.name: Authentication Feedback
